Generates a C byte array from input bytes and writes it to a file with a specified variable name.

# Purpose
The code defines a function [`write`](<#write>) that generates a C-style static array declaration from a sequence of bytes. It writes the array to a specified `output_file`, using the provided `var_name` as the name of the array. The function formats the byte values as hexadecimal and organizes them into lines, inserting comments every 16 bytes to indicate the byte range. This functionality is narrow, focusing specifically on converting byte sequences into a C-compatible format for embedding in source code.
# Functions

---
### write<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/tools/generate_c_byte_array.write}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../tools/generate_c_byte_array.py#L16>)

Writes a C-style static array definition to a file from a sequence of bytes.
- **Inputs**:
    - `output_file`: A file-like object where the function writes the output.
    - `bytes`: A sequence of bytes to be written as a C-style array.
    - `var_name`: The name of the variable to use in the C-style array definition.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Writes the initial line of the C-style array definition with the variable name.
    - Iterates over the bytes and their indices, writing each byte in hexadecimal format to the file.
    - Inserts a comment every 16 bytes to indicate the byte range.
    - Writes the closing line of the array definition.
- **Output**: No return value; writes directly to the provided file-like object.
